title: gymnadenia conopsea subsp. comigera
intro: Gymnadenia conopsea subsp. comigera, also known as the Autumn Lady's Tresses, is a subspecies of orchid characterized by its delicate white flowers with a reddish-brown labellum, typically found in mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Gymnadenia conopsea subsp. comigera include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and it can also be dried and used as a powder for medicinal purposes.
